chapter: Chapter 3 – The Making of a Global World

questions.question: What is globalisation?
questions.answer: Integration of countries via trade, people, and ideas.

questions.question: Name one ancient trade route.
What was the Silk Route famous for?
questions.answer: Trade in silk, spices, and other goods.

questions.question: Which discovery led to the beginning of the Columbian Exchange?
What was the Columbian Exchange?
questions.answer: Exchange of crops, animals, and diseases between Old and New Worlds.

questions.question: Name one food crop that came to Europe from America.
What caused the Great Irish Famine?
What is indentured labour?
questions.answer: Workers bonded by contract to work overseas.

questions.question: From which country were many indentured labourers taken to plantations?
Name one plantation colony where Indian labourers were sent.
What was the Corn Law?
questions.answer: British law restricting food grain imports.

questions.question: Why were the Corn Laws abolished?
What is meant by trade surplus?
What was the Great Depression?
Which country was most affected by the Great Depression initially?
How did the Great Depression affect Indian farmers?
What was Bretton Woods Conference?
questions.answer: 1944 meeting to plan post-war economic system.

questions.question: Which institution was created at Bretton Woods?
What does IMF stand for?
What is migration?
questions.answer: Movement of people from one place to another.

questions.question: Why did many Europeans migrate in the 19th century?
questions.answer: Poverty and search for better opportunities.

questions.question: What was the main export of India during British rule?
questions.answer: Raw materials like cotton and jute.

questions.question: What was the impact of railways on global trade?
questions.answer: Increased speed and volume of trade.

questions.question: What is meant by protectionism?
questions.answer: Policy protecting domestic industries by restricting imports.

questions.question: What was the main aim of global economic integration after World War II?
questions.answer: To ensure economic stability and growth.
